title: Fermented Tendy Brazilian Rice Stick Noodles Stir-fry
description: A low-calorie-friendly weeknight dish featuring fermented rice stick noodles stir-fried with vegetables and spices for a nutritious meal.

ingredients.name: fermented rice stick noodles
ingredients.notes: Ensure the noodles are already fermented

ingredients.name: bell peppers
ingredients.notes: Red and green mixed for color contrast

ingredients.name: tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Optional, for added flavor and color

instructions: Wash the fermented rice stick noodles thoroughly to remove any residue.
Prepare bell peppers by slicing them into thin strips and finely chop onions. Peel and mince garlic cloves.
In a large skillet or wok, heat peanut oil over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and ginger, sautéing for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chopped onions to the skillet and stir-fry for another minute until they start to soften slightly.
Fold in sliced bell peppers and continue cooking for an additional 2 minutes allowing them to release their moisture.

Reserve some of the reserved liquid from the fermented rice stick noodles. Add the noodles into the skillet, pour in soy sauce and any reserved noodle liquid to prevent sticking. Stir-fry for about 5-7 minutes until the noodles are heated through.
If using, stir in tomato paste and mix well with the noodles and vegetables. Adjust seasoning if needed based on preference or taste.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course, garnished with extra chopped garlic and scallions (if available) for added flavor.
